diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index cfdaf04..efe4b4a 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,42 +1,44 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 9 actions (Day 63)
-- Day63 before actions: cash $641,016; after starting R&D tier2 cash $307,683. Active individual subs 16,894 (S2 16,864; S1 30). Enterprise subscribed seats 0. Active stored MRR ~$1.098M/mo. Open issues 1,348; S2 issues avg age only ~0.056d, but social still has many S2 quota-wall complaints. No outage/overload; last-week peak daily usage ~1.34M vs tier3 2.5M.
-- Week 8->9 results from $8k/day S2 content ads: 7,864 paid S2 leads (~1,123/day) + ~938 S2 network leads; paid conversion ~87.7%, network conversion ~87.6%; paid plan mix ~66%A / 33%B / tiny C. Subscription receipts $445k vs weekly costs ~$139k, cash rose ~$306k despite higher ops/dev. CAC still immediately cash-positive: ad+lead cost ~<$9/sub, first payment avg ~$46 before this week’s price change.
-- Day63 actions now in force:
-  - Prices raised for NEW customers to A=$59, B=$119, C=$249. Existing subscriptions appear not repriced. Tiers A=2, B=3, C=4. Quotas raised to A=600, B=2500, C=8000 to answer quota complaints. Recurring promo $0. Lead promo still S2=$20 only; others $0. Ads strength remains 0.
-  - S2 content_marketing ads doubled again to $16k/day. `set_targeted_ad_spend` is full replacement; only content_marketing/S2 is funded.
-  - Spend: global ops $500/day + targeted S2 ops $7,000/day (total ops $7.5k/day). Global dev $1,000/day + targeted S2 dev $3,000/day (total dev $4k/day). Capacity increased to tier4 (8M units, $4k/day) to protect doubled ad scale and possible E3 acceptances.
-  - R&D: tier1 in progress t1_1, completes Day86, sampled boost +0.0289. Started tier2 t2_1 on Day63, cost $333,333, sampled completion Day93 (30d) and boost +0.0619. Do not start tier3 unless cash after week is safely >$1.0M or growth remains strongly cash-positive; tier3 cost $500k.
-  - Posted Day63 social: announced A600/B2500/C8000 headroom, burst-safe workflow alerts, expanded support/eval capacity.
+## Current strategy after Week 10 actions (Day 70)
+- Day70 before actions: cash $888,788; after starting R&D tier3 cash $388,788. Active individual subs 30,125 (S2 30,103; S1 22). Enterprise active 3 E3 customers / 2,027 seats. Active stored MRR ~$2.26M/mo (S2 ~$2.13M + E3 ~$131k). Open issues 2,367; S2 issue avg age ~0.12d, but many social complaints about quotas/rate limits. No outage/overload; last-week peak usage 2.94M vs tier4 8M.
+- Week 9->10 results with $16k/day S2 content ads and A59/B119/C249: paid S2 leads 14,512; paid conversions ~84.1% (day70 dipped to 74.8 after competitor posts); network S2 leads ~1,600; new individual subs 13,541. Subscription receipts $871.5k, costs ~$290k, net +$581k. CAC still very cash-positive: ad+lead CAC roughly <$15/sub; first payment avg ~$57 with S2 $20 lead promo; stored MRR/sub ~$77.
+- Day70 actions now in force:
+  - Prices unchanged A=$59/B=$119/C=$249; tiers A=2/B=3/C=4; S2 lead promo kept at $20; recurring promo $0; ads strength 0.
+  - Quotas raised massively to A=1,000 / B=200,000 / C=300,000 units/day. Reason: enterprise C quota 8k was per-account, not per-seat; 1152-seat and 338-seat E3 customers hit cap and immediately opened churn-prevention threads. Watch compute and enterprise usage carefully.
+  - S2 content_marketing ads raised to $24k/day. `set_targeted_ad_spend` is full replacement; only content_marketing/S2 is funded.
+  - Spend: global ops $1k/day + targeted ops S2 $10k/day + E3 $3k/day (total ops $14k/day). Global dev $2k/day + targeted dev S2 $6k/day + E3 $3k/day (total dev $11k/day). Capacity remains tier4 (8M units, $4k/day). Move to tier5 if peak usage >6.5M or large E3 acceptances arrive.
+  - R&D: t1_1 completes Day86 boost +0.0289; t2_1 completes Day93 boost +0.0619; t3_1 started Day70, cost $500k, completes Day100 boost +0.1023. Consider tier4 ($666,667) only if cash after next week is safely >$1.5M and growth/retention remain good.
+  - Posted Day70 social: announced A1k/B200k/C300k daily units, clearer burst alerts, expanded S2/enterprise support.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- IMPORTANT: The supposed weekly enterprise_autoresponder did NOT handle Day57-63 leads automatically; 20 latest system/customer turns were awaiting manual response. Always manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; do not rely on scripts/registration.
-- Day63 manual enterprise responses: replied to all 20 latest system/customer turns totaling 26,608 seats. Standard new E3 offers remain C $69 / B $49. Accepted/counter-matched customer 5618 (757 seats) with B $27.67 as first strategic low-margin pilot. After actions, latest customer/system awaiting response count = 0.
-- Total open E3 pipeline after Day63: 41 leads / ~47,861 seats. Older Day49/Day56 leads are still waiting on customers; E3 avg response previously estimated ~23.8d, so responses may start soon. Check for acceptances/counters every week before next-week.
-- E3 unit economics: noisy WTP ~$104/seat/mo, usage ~137.5 units/seat/day. At B49, model cost ~= $24.75/seat/mo; at C69, cost ~= $49.50/seat/mo, so B has better margin and C acceptable at $69. B27.67 is only small positive margin and should be rare/strategic. Avoid C prices in low $40s for large seat counts.
+- ALWAYS manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; do not rely on scripts. Docs say no response within 3 days, server allowed 6d this time, but do not risk it. Reply to every latest non-agent open thread, not just dashboard count.
+- Day70 manual responses: replied to all 24 latest non-agent turns. Standard new E3 offer remains C $69 / B $49. Special offers: customer 2156 countered C45 for 1351 seats; offered C49/B45 (avoid below-cost C45 if possible). Churn prevention: 4991 (338 seats at C42.02) offered C42.02/B39; 5282 (1152 seats at C69) and 5955 (537 seats at C69) offered C59/B49.
+- After actions, latest customer/system awaiting response count = 0. Open E3 new-lead pipeline: 58 leads / ~65,998 seats (plus churn-prevention threads for active accounts). Check acceptances/counters/churn immediately on Day77.
+- E3 unit economics: WTP noisy ~$104/seat/mo; usage estimate ~137.5 units/seat/day. At B49 tier3 cost ~= $24.75/seat/mo, margin good. At C69 tier4 cost ~= $49.50/seat/mo, margin acceptable. C49 is near breakeven; C45 likely negative at expected usage; low-C prices only strategic/rare.
 
-## Day 70 priorities
-1. Run `report.py`, `analyze_week.py`, and query latest enterprise turns immediately. Confirm no customer/system latest enterprise messages before advancing; respond within the same week.
-2. Evaluate the aggressive $16k/day S2 content scale and price increase: paid leads/day, leads per dollar, conversion after A59/B119/C249, plan mix, first-payment cash, and support/social. If paid conversion stays >70-75% and cashflow remains strong, keep $16k or consider cautious $20k-$24k; if conversion collapses or lead efficiency falls below ~0.07 leads/$, cut back to $8k-$12k and/or revisit price/promo.
-3. Watch support and quota sentiment closely. If S2 open issue avg age >0.5-1d or quota/support complaints continue, raise targeted ops further or slow ad growth. Tier4 should handle expected load; check service_day usage vs 8M.
-4. Renewals/downgrades: existing next-7-day billings before churn were only ~$111k but next-28-day scheduled rev ~$948k. Measure retention at full price and downgrade rate after quota increases.
-5. R&D/market: If Day70 cash is safely >$1M, consider tier3 R&D ($500k). If cash >$1.5M-$2M, consider tier3 plus a small number of market discovery attempts; but protect the cash-positive S2 engine first.
+## Day 77 priorities
+1. Run `report.py`, `analyze_week.py`, and query latest enterprise turns immediately. Respond to all latest non-agent E3 rows before advancing.
+2. Evaluate $24k/day S2 content scale and quotas: paid leads/day, lpd, conversion after competitor events, plan mix, CAC/first-payment cash. Keep if conversion >70% and lead efficiency >0.07 leads/$; cut to $16k-$20k if conversion or lpd collapses.
+3. Watch compute/usage after B/C quota jump, especially E3. If E3 daily usage approaches quota or tier4 peak >6.5M, move to tier5. If C low-price accounts use far above expected, avoid more low-C deals.
+4. Watch support/social: if S2 issue avg age >0.5d or quota/rate-limit complaints persist, raise ops or slow ads. If quota complaints stop and usage remains cheap, consider further ad scaling.
+5. R&D/cash: if Day77 cash >$1.5M and week was strongly cash-positive, consider starting tier4. If cash <~$1M, delay R&D and protect liquidity.
 
 ## Segment/unit-economics notes
-- S2 is still the core growth engine. Info estimate WTP ~$179/mo, usage ~$231/day, q_min ~0.37, cap ~167k. Observed usage remains much lower than estimate: last week S2 A avg ~25-110 units/day, B ~56-195/day, C ~100-217/day; near-cap users low after quota increase. Delivered quality on Day63 pre-actions: S2 A~0.778, B~0.934, C~1.037. New A59/B119 should be tested carefully but should remain acceptable if quality/quotas hold.
-- S1 is low value; no paid S1 ads/promos. S3 power users remain untargeted because high usage can be unprofitable on low plans.
-- Network effects from S2 generate E3 strategic leads. Long enterprise cycles are normal; keep offers margin-aware and use tier4+ capacity if seats accept.
+- S2 remains core engine. Info estimate WTP ~$179/mo, q_min ~0.37, market cap ~167k. Observed usage is much lower than estimate: Day70 S2 A avg 49 units (max 588), B avg 134 (max 593), C avg 188; essentially no one near old B/C caps. Delivered S2 quality Day70 before new dev accrual: A 0.835, B 1.002, C 1.114.
+- Day69/70 social showed multiple competitor quality boosts (~0.09, ~0.09, ~0.10+), likely causing day70 conversion dip. Targeted dev + R&D are meant to offset this.
+- S1 low value; no paid S1 ads/promos. S3 power users remain untargeted because base quality for S3 is too low and usage could be unprofitable.
+- Network effects from S2 generate E3 leads. Long enterprise cycles are normal; keep offers margin-aware.
 
-## Forecast assumptions submitted Day63
-- After actions, model assumptions: $16k/day S2 content, price A59/B119/C249 with S2 $20 first-period promo, conversion uncertain ~70-85%, first payment avg ~$55-$60, network leads scale with active S2, monthly retention ~90-95%, fixed daily ops/dev/capacity ~$15.5k plus ads and compute, tier2 R&D cost already paid. Enterprise is the main upside; price-shock/ad saturation/support/competitors are downside.
-- Day63 forecasts to submit: 1wk point $1.0M CI $0.55M-$1.8M; 4wk point $3.8M CI $1.8M-$7.0M; 12wk point $24M CI $8M-$55M; 26wk point $75M CI $25M-$180M.
+## Forecast assumptions submitted Day70
+- Forecast model after actions: cash starts $388.8k after t3 cost; $24k/day S2 content; S2 lead promo $20; expected paid lpd ~0.10-0.13 with conversion ~0.75-0.85; new sub first cash avg ~$57, ongoing avg ~$77. Fixed daily ops/dev/capacity+ads ~$53k plus compute/lead costs. Existing and pipeline E3 are main upside/downside; quota fix may retain E3 but can raise compute.
+- Day70 forecasts to submit: 1wk point $1.25M CI $0.75M-$2.05M; 4wk point $3.6M CI $1.8M-$6.5M (allows likely future tier4/tier5 R&D spend); 12wk point $20M CI $10M-$40M; 26wk point $65M CI $30M-$140M.
 
 ## Operating reminders / lessons
 - Every `next-week` call requires rationale + 12 cash forecast numbers: 1wk, 4wk, 12wk, 26wk point/low/high.
 - Query subscription statuses with `status IN ('subscribed','active')` defensively. Subscription table has `billing_day_mod30`, not `next_billing_day`; compute days until bill from current day mod 30. Config audit table is `config_overrides`, not `config_changes`.
+- In SQL, alias `seat_count AS seats` in joins; otherwise some outputs have omitted/ambiguous `seat_count`.
 - Lead promotions reduce first billing only; `subscriptions.effective_price` stores list/ongoin

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, random, statistics, math, json, re
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
cash0=nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger")['rows'][0]['cash']
# Small active cohorts
rows=nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) price,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day,COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE s.status IN ('subscribed','active') AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2),s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day
""")['rows']
base_small=[]
for r in rows:
    base_small.append({'n':float(r['n']), 'price':float(r['price']), 'mod':int(r['billing_day_mod30']), 'age':DAY-int(r['start_day']), 'group':r['group_id'], 'plan':r['plan'], 'newprice':float(r['price'])>=59})
# Active enterprise subscriptions
ent_subs=nm.query("""
SELECT c.customer_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) price,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day,s.seat_count AS seats
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='large'
""")['rows']
base_ent=[]
for r in ent_subs:
    base_ent.append({'cid':r['customer_id'], 'seats':float(r['seats']), 'plan':r['plan'], 'price':float(r['price']), 'mod':int(r['billing_day_mod30']), 'age':DAY-int(r['start_day']), 'at_risk': r['customer_id'] in (4991,5282,5955)})
# Open new-lead enterprise pipeline, latest offer
ent_rows=nm.query("""
WITH last AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
mx AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) first_day FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT l.customer_id, c.group_id, mx.seats, mx.first_day, t.day latest_day, t.thread_type, t.offer_json, t.turn_number, t.sender
FROM last l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN mx ON mx.customer_id=l.customer_id JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id
WHERE t.closed=0 AND c.group_id='E3' AND t.thread_type='new_lead'
""")['rows']
open_ents=[]
for r in ent_rows:
    seats=float(r['seats'] or 0)
    if seats<=10: continue
    offer=r.get('offer_json') or ''
    if '27.67' in offer:
        kind='pilot'; priceB=27.67; priceC=None
    elif '49.0' in offer and '45.0' in offer:
        kind='counter2156'; priceB=45.0; priceC=49.0
    else:
        kind='standard'; priceB=49.0; priceC=69.0
    open_ents.append({'cid':r['customer_id'],'seats':seats,'first_day':int(r['first_day'] or DAY),'latest_day':int(r['latest_day'] or DAY),'kind':kind,'priceB':priceB,'priceC':priceC})
print('cash0',round(cash0),'small_active',sum(c['n'] for c in base_small),'ent_active_seats',sum(e['seats'] for e in base_ent),'open_new_ent',len(open_ents),'open_seats',sum(e['seats'] for e in open_ents))

def pct(vals,p):
    vals=sorted(vals); return vals[int((len(vals)-1)*p)]

def choose_ent_plan(e):
    if e['kind']=='pilot': return 'B', e['priceB']
    if e['kind']=='counter2156':
        # likely C if they accept near counter, sometimes B fallback
        return ('C', e['priceC']) if random.random()<0.65 else ('B', e['priceB'])
    # Observed accepted deals have chosen C despite B option; allow some B.
    return ('C', e['priceC']) if random.random()<0.70 else ('B', e['priceB'])

def schedule_existing_enterprise_accepts(days):
    events=[]
    for e in open_ents:
        age=max(0, DAY-e['first_day'])
        if e['kind']=='pilot':
            p=max(0.45,min(0.85, random.gauss(0.68,0.10)))
        elif e['kind']=='counter2156':
            p=max(0.25,min(0.70, random.gauss(0.48,0.13)))
        else:
            p=max(0.10,min(0.55, random.gauss(0.30,0.09)))
        if random.random() < p:
            if age>=20:
                rem=max(1,int(random.gauss(5,4)))
            elif age>=12:
                rem=max(2,int(random.gauss(10,6)))
            elif age>=5:
                rem=max(4,int(random.gauss(17,8)))
            else:
                rem=max(8,int(random.gauss(26,10)))
            ev_day=DAY+rem
            if ev_day<=DAY+days:
                plan,price=choose_ent_plan(e)
                events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':e['seats'],'plan':plan,'price':price,'mod':ev_day%30})
    return events

def simulate(days=84, seed=0):
    random.seed(seed)
    cash=float(cash0)
    cohorts=[c.copy() for c in base_small]
    ents=[e.copy() for e in base_ent]
    # uncertain acquisition params after $24k/day scale
    eff=random.lognormvariate(math.log(0.113),0.28)  # leads per ad dollar before saturation
    paid_base=24000*eff
    conv_base=max(0.62,min(0.90, random.gauss(0.805,0.060)))
    net_rate=max(0.0065,min(0.0145, random.gauss(0.0100,0.0020)))
    market_cap=max(125000, random.gauss(167000,22000))
    mixA=max(0.60,min(0.80, random.gauss(0.695,0.045)))
    mixB=max(0.18,min(0.38, random.gauss(0.300,0.040)))
    if mixA+mixB>0.985: mixB=0.985-mixA
    monthly_ret=max(0.88,min(0.975, random.gauss(0.940,0.024)))
    # Enterprise events
    ent_events=schedule_existing_enterprise_accepts(days)
    # At-risk active enterprise may accept retention offer or churn quickly.
    for e in ents:
        if e.get('at_risk'):
            # decide within first week
            if e['cid']==4991:
                p_retain=random.uniform(0.65,0.90); new_price=42.02
            else:
                p_retain=random.uniform(0.60,0.86); new_price=59.0
            if random.random()<p_retain:
                e['price']=new_price
                # may downgrade to B fallback
                if random.random()<0.18:
                    e['plan']='B'; e['price']=49.0 if e['cid']!=4991 else 39.0
            else:
                e['churn_day']=DAY+random.randint(1,7)
    for d in range(DAY+1, DAY+days+1):
        active=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts)
        # market saturation: as active approaches cap, paid and organic leads fall.
        sat=max(0.05, 1-active/(market_cap*1.08))
        day_paid=max(0, random.gauss(paid_base, paid_base*0.22))*sat
        day_net=max(0, random.gauss(active*net_rate, max(10, active*net_rate*0.30)))*sat
        leads=day_paid+day_net
        # future E3 leads from S2 network, accepted after agent weekly response + enterprise lag
        e3_lambda=max(0, active*0.00010*sat)
        e3_new=max(0, int(random.gauss(e3_lambda, max(1, math.sqrt(max(1,e3_lambda))))))
        for _ in range(e3_new):
            seats=max(200, random.gauss(1150,500))
            p=max(0.10,min(0.42, random.gauss(0.28,0.08)))
            if random.random()<p:
                delay=max(10,int(random.gauss(30,11)))
                ev_day=d+delay
                if ev_day<=DAY+days:
                    plan='C' if random.random()<0.70 else 'B'
                    price=69.0 if plan=='C' else 49.0
                    ent_events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':seats,'plan':plan,'price':price,'mod':ev_day%30})
        # conversion with quotas/dev/R&D boosts; competitor shock randomness.
        cv=conv_base
        if d>=86: cv+=0.012  # tier1
        if d>=93: cv+=0.020  # tier2
        if d>=100: cv+=0.025 # tier3
        # ongoing targeted dev improves conversion gradually
        cv += min(0.05, (d-DAY)*0.002)
        if random.random()<0.006: cv-=random.uniform(0.06,0.16)  # competitor/outage/social shock
        cv=max(0.48,min(0.95, random.gauss(cv,0.040)))
        new_subs=leads*cv
        ma=max(0.56,min(0.84, random.gauss(mixA,0.035)))
        mb=max(0.12,min(0.42, random.gauss(mixB,0.030)))
        if ma+mb>0.99: mb=0.99-ma
        nA=new_subs*ma; nB=new_subs*mb; nC=max(0,new_subs-nA-nB)
        # First payments with S2 $20 lead promo.
        cash += nA*39 + nB*99 + nC*229
        cohorts.append({'n':nA,'price':59.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'A','newprice':True})
        cohorts.append({'n':nB,'price':119.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'B','newprice':True})
        if nC>0:
            cohorts.append({'n':nC,'price':249.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'C','newprice':True})
        # Enterprise acceptances.
        for ev in list(ent_events):
            if ev['day']==d:
                cash += ev['seats']*ev['price']
                ents.append({'cid':None,'seats':ev['seats'],'price':ev['price'],'plan':ev['plan'],'mod':d%30,'age':0,'at_risk':False})
                ent_events.remove(ev)
        # Churn active at-risk if lost.
        for e in ents:
            if e.get('churn_day')==d:
                e['seats']=0
        ents=[e for e in ents if e['seats']>0]
        # Small renewals.
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['age']>0 and c['mod']==d%30 and c['n']>0:
                ret=monthly_ret
                if c.get('newprice'): ret-=0.005
                if d>=86: ret+=0.007
                if d>=93: ret+=0.010
                if d>=100: ret+=0.010
                if c['group']!='S2': ret=0.70
                # occasional price/support churn
                ret=max(0.74,min(0.992, random.gauss(ret,0.024)))
                c['n'] *= ret
                rev_factor=1.0
                if c['group']=='S2' and c['plan'] in ('B','C'):
                    rev_factor=max(0.91,min(1.0, random.gauss(0.972,0.018)))
                cash += c['n']*c['price']*rev_factor
            c['age']+=1
        # Enterprise renewals.
        for e in ents:
            if e['age']>0 and e['mod']==d%30 and e['seats']>0:
                # retention improves after quota fix, but low-price C may be mixed.
                eret=0.88 if e['price']>=49 else 0.80
                if d>=100: eret+=0.04
                if random.random()<eret:
                    cash += e['seats']*e['price']
                else:
                    e['seats']=0
            e['age']+=1
        ents=[e for e in ents if e['seats']>0]
        # Costs: actions set day70.
        cash -= (24000 + 14000 + 11000 + 4000)  # ads + total ops + total dev + capacity
        cash -= leads*0.5
        # Compute. Small customers observed ~0.30-0.45 $/active/day as mix shifts to B; enterprise usage estimate 137.5 units/seat/day.
        cash -= active*max(0.25, random.gauss(0.38,0.08))
        for e in ents:
            tier_cost=0.006 if e['plan']=='B' else 0.012
            # quota fix may unlock usage; mean around E3 estimate with fat-tail.
            usage=max(40, random.gauss(137.5,45))
            cash -= e['seats']*tier_cost*usage
        # Rare outage/support/reputation shock direct cost.
        if random.random()<0.010:
            cash -= random.uniform(30000,180000)
    return cash, sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts), sum(c['n']*c['price'] for c in cohorts), sum(e['seats'] for e in ents)

for days in [7,28,84,182]:
    vals=[]; subs=[]; mrr=[]; ents=[]
    for seed in range(2500):
        v,s,m,e=simulate(days, seed+days*100000)
        vals.append(v); subs.append(s); mrr.append(m); ents.append(e)
    print(days, 'mean', round(statistics.mean(vals)), 'median', round(statistics.median(vals)), 'p025', round(pct(vals,0.025)), 'p10', round(pct(vals,0.10)), 'p90', round(pct(vals,0.90)), 'p975', round(pct(vals,0.975)), 'subs_med', round(statistics.median(subs)), 'mrr_med', round(statistics.median(mrr)), 'ent_seats_med', round(statistics.median(ents)))

import json
import novamind_api as nm

print('Day', nm.vars.current_day)

# 1) Product/package changes: fix quota-wall complaints, especially enterprise team accounts.
print('Setting prices/model tiers/quotas')
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=59, B=119, C=249)
nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=4)
nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=1000, B=200000, C=300000)
# Keep recurring discounts off; keep S2 lead discount to protect conversion/LTV while scaling.
nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={})
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1":0.0,"S2":20.0,"S3":0.0,"E1":0.0,"E2":0.0,"E3":0.0})
nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={})

# 2) Scale the working S2 acquisition engine, with more support/dev guardrails.
print('Setting spend')
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing": {"S2": 24000.0}})
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=1000.0, development=2000.0)
nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 10000.0, "E3": 3000.0})
nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 6000.0, "E3": 3000.0})

# 3) Capacity: tier4 still has large headroom (8M/day) after expected quota/unpaid growth; monitor next week.
nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=4)

# 4) Enterprise: reply to every latest non-agent open thread so no day64+ leads time out.
q = '''
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns
  WHERE message_id IN (SELECT MAX(message_id) FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY thread_id)
)
SELECT l.customer_id, l.thread_id, l.thread_type, l.turn_number, l.sender, l.day, l.seat_count AS seats, l.message_text, l.offer_json,
       c.group_id, c.persona_description
FROM latest l JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id
WHERE l.closed=0 AND l.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY l.day, l.message_id
'''
rows = nm.query(q)['rows']
print('Open non-agent enterprise rows:', len(rows))
deals = []
for r in rows:
    cid = int(r['customer_id'])
    ttype = r['thread_type']
    if ttype == 'churn_prevention':
        if cid == 4991:
            # Already deeply discounted; retain on C with quota fix, plus a cheaper B fallback.
            offers = [["C", 42.02], ["B", 39.0]]
        else:
            # At-risk $69 C accounts: meaningful concession while staying margin-aware.
            offers = [["C", 59.0], ["B", 49.0]]
    elif cid == 2156:
        # Countered at C $45. Avoid below-cost C45; offer near-breakeven C and profitable B.
        offers = [["C", 49.0], ["B", 45.0]]
    else:
        # Standard E3 month-to-month menu.
        offers = [["C", 69.0], ["B", 49.0]]
    deals.append([cid, offers])

if deals:
    print('Sending deals:', json.dumps(deals))
    res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
    print(json.dumps(res, indent=2))

# 5) R&D: tier3 is now affordable and needed after competitor quality jumps.
projects = nm.query("SELECT tier,status FROM research_projects WHERE tier=3 AND status='in_progress'")['rows']
if not projects:
    print('Starting tier3 research')
    try:
        res = nm.research.start_research_project(tier=3)
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2))
    except Exception as e:
        print('tier3 start error:', e)

# 6) Social: public response to quota/rate-limit complaints.
try:
    res = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content="We heard the quota pain. Starting today: A 1k, B 200k and C 300k daily units, clearer burst alerts, and expanded S2/enterprise support so client work doesn't die on surprise limits. Reliability and predictable scale are the product.")
    print('social:', json.dumps(res, indent=2))
except Exception as e:
    print('social post error:', e)

# Verify important settings/state.
print('Cash', nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) AS cash FROM ledger")['rows'])
print('Latest config', nm.query("SELECT * FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 1")['rows'])
print('Recent overrides', nm.query("SELECT id,day,tool_name,setting_type,settings_json FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 8")['rows'])
print('Enterprise awaiting after actions', nm.query(q)['rows'])
print('Research projects', nm.query('SELECT * FROM research_projects ORDER BY started_day,tier')['rows'])
